title: 第 32 節
en: 2. That Brahman (mind) has four feet (quarters).
1 Let him recite these three verses.

2 Both these verses had to be translated here according to their scholastic interpretation, but they had originally a totally different meaning. Even the text was altered, diva being changed to divi, sva^ to sve. The first is taken from a hymn addressed to Indra, who after conquering the dark clouds brings back the light of the sun. When he does that, then the people see again, as the poet says, the daily light of the old seed (from which the sun rises) which is lighted in heaven. The other verse belongs to a hymn addressed to the sun. Its simple meaning is : ' Seeing above the darkness (of the night) the rising light, the Sun, bright among the bright, we came towards the highest light.'
3 This is a further elucidation of Kh. Up. Ill, 14, 2.

Speech is one foot, breath is one foot, the eye is one foot, the ear is one foot — so much with reference to the body. Then with reference to the gods, Agni (fire) is one foot, Vayu (air) is one foot, Aditya (sun) is one foot, the quarters are one foot. Thus both the worship which has reference to the body, and the worship which has reference to the Devas, has been taught.
3. Speech is indeed the fourth foot of Brahman. That foot shines with Agni (fire) as its light, and warms. He who knows this, shines and warms through his celebrity, fame, and glory of countenance.
4. Breath is indeed the fourth foot of Brahman. That foot shines with Vayu (air) as its light, and warms. He who knows this, shines and warms through his celebrity, fame, and glory of countenance.
5. The eye is indeed the fourth foot of Brahman.
A That foot shines with Aditya (sun) as its light, and warms. He who knows this, shines and warms throuorh his celebrity, fame, and glory of countenance.
6. The ear is indeed the fourth foot of Brahman. That foot shines with the quarters as its light, and warms. He who knows this, shines and warms through his celebrity, fame, and glory of countenance.
Nineteenth Khanda.
i. Aditya (the sun1) is Brahman, this is the doctrine, and this is the fuller account of it : —

In the beginning this was non-existent2. It be- 1 Aditya, or the sun, had before been represented as one of the four feet of Brahman. He is now represented as Brahman, or as to be meditated on as such.
! Not yet existing, not yet developed in form and name, and therefore as if not existing.
IV PRAPA27TAKA, I KHANDA, I. 55 came existent, it grew. It turned into an egg1. The egg lay for the time of a year. The egg broke open. The two halves were one of silver, the other of gold.

2. The silver one became this earth, the golden one the sky, the thick membrane (of the white) the mountains, the thin membrane (of the yoke) the mist with the clouds, the small veins the rivers, the fluid the sea.

3. And what was born from it that was Aditya, the sun. When he was born shouts of hurrah arose, and all beings arose, and all things which they desired. Therefore whenever the sun rises and sets, shouts of hurrah arise, and all beings arise, and all things which they desire.
4. If any one knowing this meditates on the sun as Brahman, pleasant shouts will approach him and will continue, yea, they will continue.
First Kkanda 2.

1. There lived once upon a time Ganamiti Pautrayawa (the great-grandson of Ganajruta), who was a pious giver, bestowing much wealth upon the 1 Anda. instead of znda. is explained as a Vedic irregularity. A similar cosmogony is given in Manu's Law Book, I, 12 seq. See Kellgren, Mythus de ovo mundano, Helsingfors, 1849.

2 Vayu (air) and Prawa (breath) had before been represented as feet of Brahman, as the second pair. Now they are represented as Brahman, and as to be meditated on as such. This is the teaching of Raikva. The language of this chapter is very obscure, and I am not satisfied with the translation.
56 tftfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
people, and always keeping open house. He built places of refuge everywhere, wishing that people should everywhere eat of his food.

2. Once in the night some Ha^sas (flamingoes) flew over his house, and one flamingo said to another : 'Hey, Bhallaksha, Bhallaksha (short-sighted friend). The light (glory) of ^anamiti Pautraya^a has spread like the sky. Do not go near, that it may not burn thee.'
3. The other answered him : ' How can you speak of him, being what he is (a ra^anya, noble), as if he were like Raikva with the car1 ?'
4. The first replied: ' How is it with this Raikva with the car of whom thou speakest ? '

The other answered : ' As (in a game of dice) all the lower casts 2 belong to him who has conquered with the Krtta. cast, so whatever good deeds other people perform, belong to that Raikva. He who knows what he knows, he is thus spoken of by me.'

5. 6*anairuti Pautraya;za overheard this conversation, and as soon as he had risen in the morning, he said to his door-keeper (kshattW) : ' Friend, dost thou speak of (me, as if I were) Raikva with the car ?'
He replied : ' How is it with this Raikva with the car ?'

6. The king said : 'As (in a game of dice), all the lower casts belong to him who has conquered with the Knta cast, so whatever good deeds other people perform, belong to that Raikva. He who knows what he knows, he is thus spoken of by me.'
zh: 2. 梵天（心靈）具有四足（四界）。
讓他誦唸這三首詩句。

2 這兩節經文在此必須按照經學家的詮釋來翻譯，但它們原本有著完全不同的意義。連經文本身都遭到了改動，diva 被改為 divi，sva 被改為 sve。第一節取自獻給因陀羅（Indra）的讚歌，因陀羅在征服暗黑的雲彩之後，將太陽的光芒重新帶回。當他這樣做時，詩人說，人民再次看到了古老種籽（太陽由此升起）的日光，這光芒在天界中被點燃。另一節經文屬於獻給太陽的讚歌。其簡單的意義是：「在黑暗（夜晚）之上看到升起的光，即太陽，光者中最光亮者，我們朝向最高的光而來。」
3 此為《迦陀奧義書》(Kh. Up.) III, 14, 2 的進一步闡釋。
言語是一足，呼吸是一足，眼睛是一足，耳朵是一足——以上是就身體而言。然後就諸神而言，阿格尼（火）是一足，伐由（風）是一足，阿迪底耶（太陽）是一足，四方是一足。如此，既教導了就身體而言的祭祀，也教導了就諸提婆而言的祭祀。
3. 言語確實是梵天（Brahman）的第四隻腳。那隻腳以阿耆尼（Agni，火）作為其光芒而閃耀，並發熱。凡知此者，將通過其名聲、榮耀與威儀而閃耀並發熱。
4. 呼吸實是梵天（Brahman）的第四隻足。該足以風神瓦尤（Vayu）為其光而發光，並發熱。知曉此者，以其名望、聲譽及容光之榮耀而發光且發熱。
眼睛確實是梵天的第四隻腳。
那隻腳以阿迪提亞（太陽）為其光芒而閃耀，並散發溫暖。知道這一點的人，將透過其名望、榮耀與光彩而閃耀，並散發溫暖。
6. 耳確實是梵天（Brahman）的第四足。該足以四方為其光而閃耀，並散發溫暖。知曉此者，藉由其聲名、名望與容貌之光榮而閃耀，並散發溫暖。
第十九章
i. 阿迪提亞（太陽）即梵天（Brahman），此乃其教義，而此為其更完整之說明：—
在開始時，這是不存在的。²阿迪提耶（Aditya），或說太陽，在之前曾被表現為梵天（Brahman）的四足之一。現在他被表現為梵天，或應被思想為如此。¹
！尚未存在，尚未在形式與名稱中展現，因此如同不存在一般。
IV PRAPĀṬHAKA, I KHANDA, I. 55 變為存在者，它生長了。它轉化成一個卵。這個卵靜臥了一年的時間。卵裂開了。兩個一半，一個是銀質的，另一個是金質的。
銀色的部分化成了這片大地，金色的部分化成了天空，厚膜（白色的）化成了山嶺，薄膜（軛膜的）化成了霧與雲，細小的脈絡化成了河流，液體化成了海洋。
3. 由此而生者，即是阿迪提亞（Aditya），太陽也。當其誕生之時，歡呼之聲四起，一切生物皆應運而生，凡其所欲之物悉皆現前。故此，每當太陽升起與落下之時，歡呼之聲四起，一切生物皆應運而生，凡其所欲之物悉皆現前。
4. 若有人知曉此理而以太陽作為梵天（Brahman）而冥思，愉悅的歡呼聲將趨近於他，且將持續不斷，誠然，將持續不斷。
第一卷 第二章

曾經有一位名叫迦那彌提‧波多剌耶瓦（Ganamiti Pautrayawa）的人，他是迦那注多（Ganajruta）的曾孫，為人虔誠且樂善好施，將大量財富捐獻給了1 Anda。1 Anda 原作 znda，此處解釋為吠陀的不規則用法。類似的宇宙生成論見於摩奴法書（Manu's Law Book）第一卷第十二節起。參見 Kellgren, *Mythus de ovo mundano*, 赫爾辛基, 1849。
2 特尤斯（空氣）與普剌瓦（呼吸）曾先前被表述為梵天的雙足，作為第二對。如今它們被表述為梵天本身，並當作如此來冥想。這是雷克瓦的教導。本章的語言極為晦澀，我對該翻譯並不完全滿意。
56 《簡陀若奧義書》(CHHANDOGYA-UPANISHAD)
人民，並且始終敞開家門。他到處建造避難所，希望人們到處都能享用他的食物。
2. 有一次，夜間有些哈薩斯（火烈鳥）飛過他的房子，一隻火烈鳥對另一隻說：「嘿，跋羅剎叉，跋羅剎叉（近視的朋友）。舍那彌提·波特剌耶那的光芒（榮耀）如同天空般四處蔓延。不要靠近，免得它灼傷你。」
3. 另一人回答他：「你怎能像談論萊克瓦與車子（一位羅闍尼亞（ra^anya），貴族）那樣談論他呢？」
4. 第一人回答道：「你所說的那個名叫賴克瓦（Raikva）的人究竟是什麼樣的人呢？他的車子怎麼樣呢？」
另一位答覆說：「如同（在擲骰子遊戲中）所有較低的點數都歸屬於以克里塔（Krta）點數獲勝者，同樣地，其他人所行的一切善業都歸屬於那個賴克瓦（Raikva）。誰知道他所知道的，他就這樣被我所述說。」
6 那羅耶提‧波烏特剌亞（*anairuti Pautraya）；札（za）偶然聽聞了這段對話，翌晨甫起身，便對其門房（*kshattW*）說道：「友人，汝何以談及我，彷彿我乃是駕車之雷克瓦（Raikva with the car）耶？」
他回答道：「這個擁有車子的賴克瓦（Raikva）到底是怎樣的人呢？」
6. 國王說：「如同（在擲骰子遊戲中）所有較低的點數都歸屬於以『駭多』（Knta）點數獲勝的人，同樣地，無論其他人民所行的一切善業，都歸屬於那位賴克瓦（Raikva）。凡知其所知者，我即如此稱述之。」
